briefing.opening: Good morning. I will now begin the climate briefing for July 4, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Global Heatwaves and the Deepening Climate Crisis
sections.content: Global climate-related news released today once again underscores the severity of the ongoing climate crisis. Record-breaking heatwaves are sweeping across the United States and Europe. Scientific analysis indicates that the heat dome phenomenon observed in the U.S. would have been 'virtually impossible' without climate change [28, 50, 58]. Due to climate change over the past 250 years, this month, including July 4th, is projected to be one of the hottest on record [32], while in Europe, temperatures have risen by approximately 2 degrees Celsius over the past 50 years, intensifying extreme heatwaves [7]. France experienced 2,025 excess deaths during the peak of the heatwave [57], and this intense heat is even threatening the safety of World Cup matches [29, 35, 52, 58], clearly demonstrating that climate change is no longer a future problem but an ongoing catastrophe [4, 37].

sections.title: ⚠️ Ecological and Economic Ripple Effects
sections.content: Beyond human health, the climate crisis is causing severe ripple effects across ecosystems and the economy. Tropical mountain wildlife is at high risk due to the impacts of climate change [33], and paleontological studies show a reduction in the size of marine animals [36]. Australia's seagrass meadows are also under pressure from climate change [42], and the loss of natural landscapes continues, such as the destruction of California's iconic Steamer Lane [46]. Economically, 25% of French municipalities have faced disasters due to the climate crisis, confronting the double burden of worsening insurance conditions [5], and in Italy, small businesses are experiencing serial closures due to the summer heatwaves [6]. In Arizona, climate change is fueling various fires, including pocket fires [40], leading to an increasing trend in the frequency and intensity of disasters.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Efforts for Carbon Neutrality and Renewable Energy Transition
sections.content: Amidst this global crisis, domestic efforts to address the climate crisis and achieve carbon neutrality are being strengthened in various ways. Gunsan City is promoting the pilot farming of a new 'triploid oyster' variety to adapt to climate change, seeking to discover new income sources [1, 2], and Seoul City is further intensifying its efforts to respond to the climate crisis [10]. In construction sites, a competition has begun to develop composite materials to replace rebar for achieving carbon neutrality [8, 16], and Hyundai Engineering & Construction is participating in a national project to develop CO2 liquefaction processes, securing carbon-neutral technologies [19]. Furthermore, Haenam County and Jangheung County are encouraging everyday carbon neutrality through local currency rewards for eco-friendly practices via their 'Carbon Moa Tantani/Tantane' programs [9, 15, 17]. In the renewable energy sector, the Korea National Railway Authority is accelerating carbon neutrality by utilizing solar power infrastructure [13, 22], and Korea Western Power is pushing forward with large-scale offshore wind power projects, accelerating towards its renewable energy targets by 2040 [26]. KT&G's renewable energy usage has increased by approximately 300 times [21], and in Hwaseong, a 'Basic Income Energy Village' utilizing solar power has begun its first operation [24], indicating clear positive shifts towards transition. Electric vehicle sales have also surged, with EVs accounting for half of the domestic imported car market in June and Tesla and BYD dominating [27], accelerating the transition to eco-friendly transportation.

sections.title: 📰 Challenges and Opportunities in Policy and Investment Landscape
sections.content: The policy and investment environment for addressing the climate crisis still presents both challenges and opportunities. Domestically, the National Assembly and the Korea Meteorological Administration are holding discussions to deepen policy debates for scientific responses to the climate crisis [3], and while the competition for SMR (Small Modular Reactor) development is accelerating amidst increasing AI power demand and carbon neutrality pressures, institutional reform is pointed out as key [11]. Meanwhile, concerns have been raised that despite the Korean renewable energy industry being at risk of falling into foreign capital, domestic financial institutions remain passive in their investments [20], and issues in energy administration were also pointed out in the establishment and operation of Jeju's wind power complex [25]. Internationally, some countries are seeing policies that run counter to climate action, such as former President Trump winning a legal battle to remove climate change signs in national parks [31]. The World Bank has abandoned its goal of allocating 45% of its funding to developing countries for climate action [51, 60], which is expected to hinder international financial support for climate initiatives. Amidst these circumstances, activists have been permitted to sue Sweden over its failure to address climate change [44], increasing demands for accountability in climate policy.

sections.title: 🌱 International Climate Change Response and Awareness Spreading
sections.content: In the international community, various efforts and awareness-raising activities to address the climate crisis are ongoing. In Wales, a scientist is working to restore wetlands (peatlands) in response to climate change [30, 55], and international experts have put forward proposals for eco-friendly architecture aiming for carbon neutrality [18]. In Shanghai, an interactive art installation in the form of a suspended glacier is raising public awareness by depicting climate change [38], and the Knowles-Nelson Stewardship Program is also contributing to combating climate change [53]. Although some experts suggest that AI can only play a 'supportive' role in climate change response [49], research indicating that goats could be the best protein for climate-adapted pet food [41] shows the potential for exploring new adaptation strategies. These efforts will be crucial steps in mitigating the impacts of climate change globally and preparing for the future.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted the global intensification of the climate crisis, while also shedding light on various domestic and international efforts and policy challenges to overcome it. This is a time that calls for sustained attention and action. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.original_title: US heatwave threatens 250th anniversary events and World Cup
articles.description: Weekend’s high temperatures and humidity ‘virtually impossible’ without climate crisis, researchers sayThe scorching heat blanketing much of the US this week would have been “virtually impossible” if not for the climate crisis, researchers have found, warning that the high temperatures could threaten Independence Day celebrations and World Cup matches this weekend.“The climate the country has today is fundamentally different to the one it had when the founding fathers signed the Declaration of I

summary.phenomenon: Intense heat and high humidity have impacted most of the United States this week. Researchers indicate that these high temperatures would have been 'virtually impossible' without the climate crisis, potentially threatening Independence Day celebrations and World Cup matches this weekend.
summary.cause: Researchers have attributed the current high temperatures and humidity in the U.S. to the climate crisis, asserting that such conditions would have been 'virtually impossible' otherwise.
summary.outlook: The high temperatures anticipated in the U.S. this weekend could pose a threat to Independence Day celebrations and World Cup matches.
